query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Nuzzle', 'To touch someone or something with the nose', 'Critics argued that the plan might nuzzle the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oaken', 'Made from the wood of the oak tree', 'Her oaken became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oakum', 'A material used to caulk or pack joints', 'Critics argued that the plan might oakum the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obdurate', 'Stubbornly refusing to change one’s opinion or course of action', '“Despite the evidence, he remained obdurate in his stance.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obeisance', 'Demonstration of an obedient attitude', 'Scholars noted a striking obeisance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obelisk', 'A tapered, stone monolith topped with a pyramidal point', 'The essay highlights the obelisk of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obese', 'Extremely overweight', 'The passage presents a obese portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obfuscate', 'To make something unclear or difficult to understand', '“The lawyer tried to obfuscate the details of the case.”', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obfuscation', 'The act of obscuring the perception of something', 'The essay highlights the obfuscation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obituary', 'A brief notice of a person’s death', 'The essay highlights the obituary of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Objective', 'Not influenced by personal feelings', 'The passage presents a objective portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Objector', 'A person who objects to something', 'Critics argued that the plan might objector the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oblig', 'Required to do something by law, duty, or necessity', 'Critics argued that the plan might oblig the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obligate', 'To compel by a social, legal, or moral tie', 'The author hoped the new policy would obligate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oblique', 'Indirect or not straightforward', '“His oblique answer made it hard to determine his true intentions.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obliterate', 'To remove or destroy all traces of, do away with', 'The author hoped the new policy would obliterate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oblivion', 'The state of being completely forgotten or unknown', 'The essay highlights the oblivion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oblivious', 'Lacking consciousness or awareness of something', 'The reviewer described the tone as oblivious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oblong', 'Describing something that is longer than it is wide', 'Scholars noted a striking oblong between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obloquy', 'Censure, blame, or abusive language aimed at something', 'The essay highlights the obloquy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obnoxious', 'Very annoying, offensive, odious or contemptible', 'What seemed obnoxious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obscure', 'Not well known or difficult to understand', '“The professor referenced an obscure 18th-century philosopher.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obsequious', 'Excessively submissive or eager to please', '“The obsequious assistant praised her boss at every opportunity.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obsequy', 'Funeral rites', 'The essay highlights the obsequy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Observant', 'Alert and paying close attention', 'The essay highlights the observant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obsession', 'A compulsive or irrational preoccupation', 'The essay highlights the obsession of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obsolescence', 'The state of being obsolete—no longer in use, disused', 'Scholars noted a striking obsolescence between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obsolete', 'No longer in use or out of date', '“VCRs have become obsolete with the rise of streaming services.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obstetrics', 'The care of women during and after pregnancy', 'Her obstetrics became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obstinacy', 'The state, or an act, of stubbornness or doggedness', 'Scholars noted a striking obstinacy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obstinate', 'Stubbornly refusing to change one’s mind', '“The child was obstinate about not eating his vegetables.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obstreperous', 'Noisy and difficult to control', 'The passage presents a obstreperous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obstruct', 'Block (an opening, path, road, etc.); be or get in the way of', 'Her obstruct became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obtrude', 'To impose (something) on someone or into some area', 'The author hoped the new policy would obtrude tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obtrusive', 'Noticeable in an unwelcome or intrusive way', '“His loud behavior was obtrusive in the quiet library.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obtuse', 'Lacking quickness of sensibility or intellect', 'Her obtuse became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obvert', 'To turn so as to show another side', 'Over time, the community learned to obvert its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Obviate', 'To anticipate and prevent or eliminate by effective measures', 'Over time, the community learned to obviate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Occident', 'That part of the earth towards the sunset, the west', 'The passage presents a occident portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Occlude', 'To close, shut, or stop up (a passage, opening, etc )', 'Scholars noted a striking occlude between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Occult', 'Any system claiming use or knowledge of secret forces', 'The essay highlights the occult of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Occupant', 'A person who occupies a place or a position', 'The essay highlights the occupant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Occupy', 'Take possession or control of a place or thing', 'The essay highlights the occupy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Occurrence', 'Actual instance where a situation arises', 'Her occurrence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Octagon', 'A polygon with eight sides and eight angles', 'Her octagon became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Octave', 'An interval spanning eight degrees of the diatonic scale', 'Scholars noted a striking octave between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Octavo', 'A book printed on sheets folded to form 8 leaves or 16 pages', 'Her octavo became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Octogenarian', 'Being between the age of 80 and 89, inclusive', 'Her octogenarian became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ocular', 'Of, or relating to the eye, or the sense of sight', 'The author hoped the new policy would ocular tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oculist', 'An ophthalmologist', 'Her oculist became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
